summary: Sprint 1 fixed the SQL guard, input validation, CORS, and logging. However, backend test coverage remains critically low at ~13% (25 tests in a single file covering only _validate_select_only). No tests exist for main.py endpoints, schemas.py, config.py, database.py, or the data pipeline. Alembic is listed in requirements.txt but never initialized -- create_all() is still the only schema management. The ingest_batting.py still uses row-by-row iterrows() while ingest_pitching.py was written with batch executemany. Two TODO stubs remain in text_to_sql.py. No conftest.py or pytest configuration exists.

findings.title: Backend test coverage at 13% -- only SQL guard tested
findings.detail: 25 tests exist but all test _validate_select_only only. No tests for: POST /api/query endpoint (success, 400, 501, 500 paths), GET /health, QueryRequest validation (min_length/max_length), generate_sql() NotImplementedError, generate_answer() stub, execute_sql() with mocked DB. Coverage target is 80% but current is ~13%.
findings.recommendation: Create backend/tests/conftest.py with async test fixtures (httpx.AsyncClient + TestClient), then add test_main.py (5 endpoint tests), test_schemas.py (validation edge cases), expand test_text_to_sql.py with execute_sql tests using mocked AsyncSession.

findings.title: No pytest configuration (conftest.py, pyproject.toml [tool.pytest])
findings.detail: No conftest.py for shared fixtures, no pyproject.toml or pytest.ini for test settings (asyncio_mode, test paths, coverage config). Tests run but without proper async support -- needed for testing async endpoints and DB operations.
findings.recommendation: Add backend/pyproject.toml with [tool.pytest.ini_options] asyncio_mode='auto', testpaths=['tests']. Add conftest.py with app fixture using httpx.ASGITransport for async endpoint testing. Add pytest-asyncio and pytest-cov to requirements.txt.

findings.title: Alembic in requirements.txt but never initialized
findings.detail: alembic==1.14.1 is installed but no alembic/ directory, alembic.ini, or migration files exist. Schema is managed by create_all() in main.py lifespan. This means: (1) schema changes require recreating tables, (2) no migration history, (3) production deployments are risky. This was flagged in Sprint 1 but not addressed.
findings.recommendation: Run alembic init alembic in backend/, configure env.py for async, create initial migration with --autogenerate, replace create_all() with alembic upgrade head in docker-compose.

findings.title: ingest_batting.py still uses row-by-row iterrows() for stats upsert
findings.detail: ingest_pitching.py uses batch executemany pattern but ingest_batting.py still iterates row-by-row with dynamic SQL construction per row. This is an inconsistency -- same pattern should be used in both scripts. The row-by-row version is also 20-30x slower for large datasets.
findings.recommendation: Refactor ingest_batting.py load() to match ingest_pitching.py's bulk upsert pattern: stats.where(stats.notna(), None).to_dict('records') + single conn.execute().

findings.title: No frontend tests at all
findings.detail: Zero test files in frontend/src/. No testing framework configured (no jest, vitest, or playwright in devDependencies). The page.tsx component has 189 lines of untested UI logic including form submission, chip clicks, error handling, and loading states.
findings.recommendation: Add vitest + @testing-library/react to devDependencies. Create __tests__/page.test.tsx with tests for: form submission, chip click auto-submit, error display, loading skeleton visibility, results table rendering.

findings.title: Duplicate player upsert logic in both ingest scripts
findings.detail: Both scripts have identical player extraction, name splitting, and upsert logic (~20 lines duplicated). PLAYER_COLUMN_MAP is identical. The player upsert SQL is identical in both files.
findings.recommendation: Extract shared code into data_pipeline/common.py: upsert_players() function, shared PLAYER_COLUMN_MAP, DATABASE_URL setup, transform_players() helper.

findings.title: No type annotations on data pipeline functions
findings.detail: load() and main() functions lack full type annotations. The coding style rules require type annotations on all function signatures.
findings.recommendation: Add type annotations: load(players: pd.DataFrame, stats: pd.DataFrame) -> None, main() -> None.

findings.title: database.py has no connection pool configuration
findings.detail: create_async_engine() uses defaults only. No pool_size, max_overflow, pool_timeout, pool_recycle, or pool_pre_ping. Should be explicitly configured for production.
findings.recommendation: Add pool_size=5, max_overflow=10, pool_timeout=30, pool_recycle=1800, pool_pre_ping=True.
